query: the world's tallest jain statue is located in which state

documents.text: [Solved] The world's tallest Jain statue is located in which - Testbook The impressive statue is located atop Mangi Tungi mountain near Teharabad village of Baglan tehsil in Nashik district of Maharashtra. Answer (Detailed Solution Below) Detailed Solution A 108-feet tall idol of Lord Rishabhdeva, the first Teerthankar of Jains, carved out of a single rock, has entered the “Guinness World Records” as the world’s tallest Jain statue. The impressive statue is located atop Mangi Tungi mountain near Teharabad village of Baglan tehsil in Nashik district of Maharashtra. Before this statue, the 57 feet idol of Lord Bahubali in Shravanabelagola in Karnataka was considered the world’s tallest Jain statue. Additional Information ​The Statue of Unity is the tallest statue in the world, with a height of 182 meters (597 ft), located near Kevadia in the Indian state of Gujarat.

Do You Know Where The World's Tallest Jain Statue is Located? Located in the Nashik District of Maharashtra, the 'Statue of Ahimsa' stands 128 feet tall (including its pedestal) and holds the Guinness ... Statue of Ahimsa holds the Guinness record for world's tallest Jain idol. Here's all that you should know about it. Jainism is one of the most prominent religions of India. Today is Mahavir Jayanti 2018, a day that marks the birth anniversary of Jainisms last Tirthankara (spiritual teacher), Lord Mahavir. As such, it is a significant day for the Jain community all over the world. Most Mahavir Jayani celebration rituals include a procession carrying the idol of Lord Mahavir from one religious spot to another with devotees in presence. On this day, devotees visit a Jain temple and pay their respects. India is home to a number of Jain temples that are worth a visit. However, did you know that the worlds tallest Jain statue is located in the state of Maharashtra? ALSO SEE 5 Jain Temples in Maharashtra You Must Visit Located in the Nashik District of Maharashtra, the Statue of Ahimsa stands 128 feet tall (including its pedestal) and holds the Guinness record for worlds tallest Jain idol. It is 1840 sq feet in size. The humongous idol was carved out of the Mangi Tungi hills in the Teharabad village of Baglan tehsil. It is a statue of Lord Rishabhdeva, the first Jain Tirthankar. The Mangi Tungi hills are a significant pilgrim site for the Jain community as they are one of the 4 siddha kshetras of the religion. The Statue of Ahimsa is on top of the hill at an altitude of 4,343 feet above sea level.

Gommateshwara statue - Wikipedia The Gommateshwara statue is a 57-foot (17 m) high monolithic statue on Vindhyagiri, also known as Indra-giri in the town of Shravanbelagola in the Indian state ... The Gommateshwara statue is dedicated to the Jain figure Bahubali and symbolises the Jain precepts of peace, non-violence, sacrifice of worldly affairs, and simple living. It was built around 983 CE during the Western Ganga dynasty and is one of the largest free-standing statues in the world.[3] It was regarded the tallest Jain statue until 2016.[4] The construction of the statue was commissioned by the Ganga dynasty minister and commander Chavundaraya. Neighbouring areas have Jain temples known as basadis and several images of the Tirthankaras. Vindyagiri Hill is one of the two hills in Shravanabelagola. The other is Chandragiri, which is also a seat of several ancient Jain centres, much older than Gommateshwara statue. Chandragiri is dedicated to the Jain figure Bharat, the brother of Bahubali and the son of the first Tirthankara Rishabhnath. In 2007, the statue was voted the first of Seven Wonders of India in a Times of India poll; 49% of the total votes went in favour of it.[7] The Archaeological Survey of India has listed the Gommateshwara statue in a group of monuments in Shravanabelagola known as the Adarsh Smarak Monument.[8] Iconography The statue depicts the prolonged meditation of Bahubali. The motionless contemplation in kayotsarga (standing still) posture led to the growth of climbing vines around his legs.[9] The nagna (nude) image of Gommateshwara has curly hair ringlets and large ears. The eyes are half open, sight rested on nose showcasing his detachment to look on the world. His facial features are perfectly chiseled with a faint touch of a smile at the corner of the lips that embodies a calm inner peace and vitality. His shoulders are broad, the arms stretch straight down and the figure has no support from the thigh upwards. There is an anthill in the background which signifies his incessant penance.

The Statue of Ahimsa, located at Mangi-Tungi in Nashik ... - Facebook The Statue of Ahimsa, located at Mangi-Tungi in Nashik, Maharashtra, is the world's tallest Jain statue and a remarkable feat of engineering and spirituality. Rajendra Parekh's Post The Statue of Ahimsa, located at Mangi-Tungi in Nashik, Maharashtra, is the world’s tallest Jain statue and a remarkable feat of engineering and spirituality. This colossal monument depicts Rishabhanatha, the first Jain Tirthankara, and stands at an impressive height of 108 feet (33 meters), with a total height of 121 feet (37 meters) including its pedestal. The Mangi-Tungi hills have deep religious significance for Jains. The area is dotted with numerous temples and caves, many of which are named after Jain Tirthankaras such as Mahavira, Rishabhanatha, Shantinatha, and Parshvanatha. The site is considered a Siddha Kshetra, meaning a gateway to enlightenment. The ascent to the statue involves climbing approximately 3,500 steps, offering pilgrims and tourists a challenging but spiritually rewarding journey. The site provides breathtaking views of the surrounding landscape and a serene atmosphere conducive to meditation and reflection. The Statue of Ahimsa at Mangi-Tungi has become a significant pilgrimage site for Jains worldwide and a notable tourist attraction, showcasing the rich cultural and architectural heritage of Jainism in India.

baseline.final_answer: The world's tallest Jain statue is located in Maharashtra.
